name: French Rabbit Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 clove Garlic, minced (it really needs 2 or 3)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean, wash, and dry the rabbit.
2. Melt the butter in a Dutch oven over medium heat.
3. Brown the rabbit in the butter, then sprinkle with salt (optional), pepper, and flour, stirring until the flour browns.
4. Add beef broth and white wine; bring to a boil, cover, and cook on low heat for 45 minutes to one hour or until tender.
5. While the rabbit is cooking, brown the bacon lightly in a skillet over medium heat. Pour off half the fat.
6. Add onions to the skillet and sauté until golden. Add garlic and mushrooms; sauté for an additional 3 minutes.
7. Stir the onion mixture into the rabbit pot and cook for another 15 minutes or until the rabbit is tender.
8. If the mixture seems dry, add a little more beef broth or white wine while cooking.
Note: The recipe can be adjusted based on personal preference for seasoning and cooking times.
